text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real yet-another-react-lightbox repository at the commit where the feature gap below needs closing. Implement the feature in the library source.

Context: this library renders a photo/video lightbox carousel. It ships a Zoom plugin that adds pinch/scroll/double-click zoom interactions to the currently displayed slide. Right now the Zoom plugin only ever recognizes image slides -- internally it decides whether to wrap a slide in its interactive zoom container purely by checking whether that slide is an image slide, with no way to opt any other kind of slide into zoom. Consumers can render arbitrary custom slide types through the lightbox's `render.slide` render-prop (keyed by a slide's `type` field, e.g. a custom map view, a video player, a PDF page, etc.), but none of those custom slide types can ever get zoom behavior, no matter what the consumer configures -- the plugin just renders them completely unwrapped, with no zoom UI, no pinch/scroll/double-click handling, nothing.

Feature request: add a way for a consumer to opt specific custom slide types into the same zoom wrapper/gesture handling that image slides already get. Concretely, the Zoom plugin's options object (the `zoom` prop passed to the lightbox) should grow two new optional properties:

- `supports`: a list of slide `type` strings. When the currently displayed slide's `type` is included in this list, the plugin should wrap it in the same interactive zoom container an image slide gets (so it participates in zoom in/out, pinch, scroll-to-zoom, double-click/double-tap, etc., exactly like an image slide does) -- even though the slide itself isn't an image slide. A slide whose `type` is not image and not listed in `supports` (or when `supports` is not configured at all) should continue to render completely unwrapped, exactly as today.

- `maxZoom`: controls how far a non-image slide can be zoomed in. For image slides the maximum zoom level is already computed automatically from the image's natural resolution vs. its displayed size, but that computation is meaningless for a non-image custom slide (there's no natural image resolution to compare against), so it needs its own configurable ceiling. `maxZoom` may be a plain number, or a function that receives the current slide and returns either a number or `undefined`; returning `undefined` (or omitting `maxZoom` entirely) should fall back to a sensible default maximum zoom level of 8x. This setting only affects non-image slides -- image slides keep using their existing resolution-based maximum zoom calculation unchanged.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (Node, npm). You can scope the existing Vitest suite to whatever file you're iterating on with `npx vitest run <path-to-file>`. Implement the feature in the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

text: I’m tracing the Zoom plugin’s slide-type detection, option typings, and existing tests first so the new opt-in path follows the library’s current APIs and preserves image behavior.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc 'pwd && rg --files | head -80 && rg -n "supports|maxZoom|Zoom|zoom" src test* __tests__ 2>/dev/null | head -240'

text: The relevant split is clear: `Zoom.tsx` decides whether a slide is wrapped, while `ZoomWrapper` and `ZoomController` carry the maximum-zoom state. I’m checking those contracts and the test harness now, especially how custom `render.slide` output is represented and whether the wrapper assumes an image element.

text: The wrapper itself can already host arbitrary React content, but two image-only assumptions prevent useful behavior: the controller reports zoom as disabled unless the current slide has an image `src`, and the geometry hook returns a `1x` ceiling for non-images. I’ll make the active wrapper establish availability, use the configured non-image ceiling with an `8x` fallback, and retain the existing image-resolution path untouched.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "git status --short && git log -5 --oneline && sed -n '1,120p' src/plugins/video/index.ts && sed -n '1,110p' src/index.ts && sed -n '1,100p' src/plugins/index.ts && sed -n '1,100p' src/plugins/zoom/hooks/useZoomProps.ts"

text: I’m going to update the public Zoom option declaration, the wrapper-selection predicate, and the controller geometry/state hooks. For custom slides, the zoomable content area will use the slide rectangle as its base geometry, so panning and scaling behave consistently; image slides will continue through their natural/source-dimension calculation.

The first combined patch hit a context mismatch and made no changes. I’ve narrowed the edits to exact file regions; the implementation remains the same, with an explicit guard for the no-current-slide state so the new resolver stays type-safe.

text: The focused test passes and ESLint is clean. I’m tightening one compatibility detail before broader verification: image slides with a missing/empty source should retain their old disabled behavior, while only an actually active custom wrapper enables zoom.

text: The source-level checks are running now: the library JS build, the type-consumer fixture, and whitespace/lint validation. This will also confirm that the function-valued `maxZoom` declaration survives the generated public typings.

The JS build succeeds. The repository’s type fixture fails on pre-existing export/type errors in `test/types/src/App.tsx` (including its existing `ZoomRef` import), unrelated to these edits; I’m adding focused Zoom coverage next so the opt-in wrapping, default/custom ceilings, and non-opted-out behavior are exercised directly.

The test file is smaller than the surrounding patterns I initially assumed, so that test patch did not apply and left the worktree unchanged. I’ve re-read its exact contents and am applying the coverage against that structure now.

Node.js v22.13.0

text:
Implemented custom-slide zoom support.

Changes:
- Added `zoom.supports` and `zoom.maxZoom` options.
- Custom slides in `supports` now receive the full Zoom wrapper and gestures.
- Non-image slides default to `8x`, with number/function overrides.
- Image zoom behavior remains resolution-based.
- Added focused tests for supported, unsupported, and custom max zoom behavior.

Verification: 78 unit tests pass, build and lint pass. The type fixture still has unrelated pre-existing export errors.
